# jsoup Contribution Guide

Thanks for your interest in contributing to jsoup. Please follow these guidelines:

## Issues
- When reporting an issue, include details of your run time environment (platform, JDK version, etc as appropriate) and which version of jsoup you are using.
- Make sure you provide a simple runnable test case that demonstrates the bug.
- Make sure you are testing against the latest version of jsoup.

## Pull Requests
- Before raising a PR it is best to start a [discussion](https://github.com/jhy/jsoup/discussions) first, so we can align on the shape of the API / feature / design concept etc.
- Keep commits focussed just on the specific change; don't reformat / change imports etc.
- PRs that are just general refactors won't be accepted unless it's been explicitly discussed previously.
- By submitting a PR you positively confirm that you are the sole author of the work and that you are offering it to the project under the terms of the [license](https://github.com/jhy/jsoup/blob/master/LICENSE).

## Use of AI
- In the jsoup project, we do not accept code that has been generated in whole or part by AI/LLMs. There are many other projects in the world that do; if this is your interest please see elsewhere.
- Similarly, please don't submit in Issues, PRs, or Discussions copy that was written by AI, or is otherwise not your own writing. (If you are submitting a machine translation to English, please include your original pre-translated text as well.)
